description: Clean up version-info.ts: simplify getCliVersion() to use the same one-level-up pattern as cli.ts, fix the broken fallback path, fix getMcpServerVersion() for global installs by adding ./package.json export to mcp-server, and add unit tests so this path resolution logic is never untested again.

userStories.title: Simplify getCliVersion() and fix getMcpServerVersion() fallback path
userStories.description: Simplify getCliVersion() to use join(cliDir, '..', 'package.json') matching the proven pattern in cli.ts. Remove the unnecessary import.meta.resolve + try/catch. Fix getMcpServerVersion() by adding ./package.json export to mcp-server/package.json so import.meta.resolve works, and fix the fallback path. Remove the now-unnecessary ./package.json export from cli/package.json.
acceptanceCriteria: getCliVersion() uses `join(cliDir, '..', 'package.json')` with no try/catch or import.meta.resolve
getMcpServerVersion() primary path uses import.meta.resolve('@opentabs-dev/mcp-server/package.json') which succeeds because the export exists
getMcpServerVersion() fallback path resolves to a valid location in monorepo dev (platform/mcp-server/package.json)
platform/cli/package.json exports field does NOT include './package.json' (no longer needed)

userStories.title: Add unit tests for version-info.ts
userStories.description: Create platform/cli/src/version-info.test.ts that tests the real getCliVersion() and getMcpServerVersion() implementations (no mocking). This ensures the path resolution logic is validated and regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/cli/src/version-info.test.ts exists and is covered by tsconfig.test.json
getCliVersion() test calls the real function and asserts it returns a valid semver string matching the version in platform/cli/package.json
getMcpServerVersion() test calls the real function and asserts it returns a valid semver string matching the version in platform/mcp-server/package.json
All tests pass with npm run test
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/version-info.test.ts (new file)

Context:
version-info.ts had zero test coverage — the only test file that references it (doctor.test.ts) mocks the entire module with `vi.mock('../version-info.js')` at line 24. The path resolution bug shipped because no test ever called the real implementation.

The test file will be automatically included by `platform/cli/tsconfig.test.json` which has `"include": ["src/**/*.test.ts"]`.

Changes:
Create `platform/cli/src/version-info.test.ts` with:

1. A test for getCliVersion() that:
   - Calls the real `getCliVersion()` (no mocking)
   - Asserts the result matches `/^\d+\.\d+\.\d+/` (semver)
   - Reads `platform/cli/package.json` independently and asserts the version matches

2. A test for getMcpServerVersion() that:
   - Calls the real `getMcpServerVersion()` (no mocking)
   - Asserts the result is not null (since mcp-server is installed in the monorepo)
   - Asserts the result matches `/^\d+\.\d+\.\d+/` (semver)
   - Reads `platform/mcp-server/package.json` independently and asserts the version matches

Use `import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises'` and `import { dirname, join } from 'node:path'` and `import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'` to read the package.json files independently for comparison. Use `dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))` to get the test file's directory, then resolve `../../package.json` for the CLI package.json (test file is at `src/version-info.test.ts`, package.json is at `platform/cli/package.json` — but since test runs from source, the dir is `platform/cli/src/`, so `../package.json` is correct).

Constraints:
- Do NOT mock anything — the whole point is to test the real path resolution
- Do NOT import from doctor.test.ts or reuse its patterns — that file mocks version-info
- Use `describe`/`it` from vitest, and `expect` for assertions
